title: Spectral Trainer: Learning-Rate Schedule Design
strategies: conservative constant step eta=1/L (reproduces the evaluator's weak baseline; barely reduces slow modes -> ~0.1)
optimal constant step eta=2/(L+mu) for the quadratic (contraction rate (kappa-1)/(kappa+1) per step; beats 1/L but no acceleration; can oscillate on edge-heavy spectra)

Chebyshev step schedule: reciprocals of the roots of the degree-N minimax residual polynomial on [mu,L] -> accelerated rate (sqrt(kappa)-1)/(sqrt(kappa)+1); near-optimal worst-case, ignores gradient weights

per-instance polynomial optimization: weight the residual polynomial by the actual gradient components g_i (weighted-Chebyshev / least-squares fit or coordinate descent on the step sizes) to beat interval-Chebyshev on the discrete weighted spectrum, approaching the headroom ceiling
